script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'phase2c-checkpoint',
  description: 'Turn on the generation checkpoint (2C): save pre-check data only, gate write behind PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED so flag-off runs stay byte-identical, reload re-verifies from scratch',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Setup', detail: 'worktree + study checkpoint_manager.py + the generation loop' },
    { title: 'Wire', detail: 'save pre-check data (gated by flag) + reload path (re-verify from scratch)' },
    { title: 'Validate', detail: 'flag-off oracle byte-identical + collection unchanged + flag-on save/reload roundtrip test' },
    { title: 'Codex-Gate', detail: 'codex confirms flag-off byte-identical + no verdict persisted' },
    { title: 'Record', detail: 'commit + push + PR' },
  ],
}
const PY = '/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python'
const GOLDEN = '9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98'
const WT = '/home/polaris/wt/phase2c'

phase('Setup')
const setup = await agent(
  `Set up to wire the generation checkpoint (Plan V4 item 2C) in a research pipeline.
1. cd /workspace/POLARIS && git worktree remove ${WT} --force 2>/dev/null; git branch -D chore/review-readiness-phase2c 2>/dev/null; git worktree add ${WT} -b chore/review-readiness-phase2c chore/review-readiness-phase1.
2. Overlay the pinned oracle for validation: cd ${WT} && git checkout chore/review-readiness-phase0 -- tests/oracle/ 2>/dev/null; cp -n /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/*.jsonl /home/polaris/wt/phase0/tests/oracle/cassettes/acceptance_golden.json ${WT}/tests/oracle/cassettes/ 2>/dev/null; ls ${WT}/tests/oracle/cassettes/.
3. STUDY the checkpoint mechanism: read src/polaris_graph/**/checkpoint_manager.py fully (find it). Understand: what does it save/load, what is PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED (default '0'), is the save/reload actually WIRED into the generation loop or is it a dormant module?
4. Find the GENERATION LOOP where a checkpoint should save (after drafts/outline are produced but BEFORE the faithfulness/strict_verify check — 'pre-check data only, never a verdict') and where it should reload (on restart, re-verify from scratch). Identify the exact functions/files + the current wiring state.
5. Baseline: 'cd ${WT} && timeout 200 ${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q 2>&1 | tail -1' (expect 16738/11).
Return: checkpoint_manager location + capabilities, whether save/reload is already wired or dormant, the exact save-point and reload-point in the generation loop, and the baseline.`,
  { label: 'setup', phase: 'Setup', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['checkpoint_manager','wiring_state','save_point','reload_point','baseline'], properties:{ checkpoint_manager:{type:'string'}, wiring_state:{type:'string'}, save_point:{type:'string'}, reload_point:{type:'string'}, baseline:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Wire')
const wire = await agent(
  `Wire the generation checkpoint in ${WT} per Plan V4 2C + the safety rules. Context from setup: checkpoint_manager=${JSON.stringify(setup.checkpoint_manager)}; wiring_state=${JSON.stringify(setup.wiring_state)}; save_point=${JSON.stringify(setup.save_point)}; reload_point=${JSON.stringify(setup.reload_point)}.
REQUIREMENTS (hard):
1. SAVE PRE-CHECK DATA ONLY — drafts + outline + retrieved-evidence needed to resume, NEVER a faithfulness/strict_verify VERDICT (a resumed run must re-verify from scratch, so a poisoned/partial verdict can never be trusted from disk).
2. The WRITE must be gated behind PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED (default '0'/off). When the flag is OFF, the generation path must be BYTE-IDENTICAL to today — no checkpoint file written, no new branch taken, no changed ordering. Use the config accessor already in the codebase (resolve('PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED') or os.getenv) — do NOT change its default.
3. The RELOAD path (on restart with the flag on + a checkpoint present): load the pre-check drafts/outline, then RUN THE FAITHFULNESS/STRICT_VERIFY CHECK AGAIN from scratch (never trust a saved verdict).
4. If checkpoint_manager already implements save/load correctly, you may only need to WIRE the call sites (guarded by the flag); reuse it, don't rewrite.
Keep the change minimal and localized. py_compile all changed files. After wiring, confirm collection stays at baseline (${setup.baseline}).
Return: files changed, exactly where the flag-gated save + reload were added, and confirmation that flag-OFF takes no new code path (the guard wraps the entire checkpoint write).`,
  { label: 'wire', phase: 'Wire', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['files_changed','save_wiring','reload_wiring','flag_off_is_noop','collection_after'], properties:{ files_changed:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, save_wiring:{type:'string'}, reload_wiring:{type:'string'}, flag_off_is_noop:{type:'boolean'}, collection_after:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Validate')
const validate = await agent(
  `Validate the checkpoint wiring in ${WT} is byte-identical when OFF and functional when ON.
1. FLAG-OFF byte-identical (the critical gate): 'cd ${WT} && PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS=3 ${PY} tests/oracle/acceptance_portable.py --replay 2>&1 | tail -12' with PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED unset/0 — artifact SHA MUST equal ${GOLDEN}, both controls pass. (Proves normal runs are unchanged.)
2. Collection unchanged: '${PY} -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q | tail -1' == ${setup.baseline}.
3. Config characterization: '${PY} -m pytest tests/test_config_registry.py tests/test_settings_models.py -q | tail -1'.
4. FLAG-ON roundtrip: write a NEW test (tests/test_checkpoint_roundtrip.py) that, with PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED=1, exercises the checkpoint save then reload and asserts: (a) a checkpoint artifact is written containing drafts/outline, (b) it contains NO faithfulness/strict_verify verdict field, (c) the reload path re-runs verification (does not read a verdict from disk). Run it and report pass/fail. Keep the test hermetic (no network — stub/monkeypatch as needed).
Return all gate outcomes + the oracle SHA + whether the new roundtrip test passes + confirmation no verdict is persisted.`,
  { label: 'validate', phase: 'Validate', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['flag_off_oracle_sha','flag_off_byte_identical','collection_ok','characterization_ok','roundtrip_test_passes','no_verdict_persisted','notes'], properties:{ flag_off_oracle_sha:{type:'string'}, flag_off_byte_identical:{type:'boolean'}, collection_ok:{type:'boolean'}, characterization_ok:{type:'boolean'}, roundtrip_test_passes:{type:'boolean'}, no_verdict_persisted:{type:'boolean'}, notes:{type:'string'} } } })

phase('Codex-Gate')
const gate = await agent(
  `Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate the checkpoint wiring. Write /tmp/ckpt_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 240 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/ckpt_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -30' (embed evidence inline; medium effort; no sandbox flags; bubblewrap warning fine).
Context: wired the generation checkpoint. wire=${JSON.stringify(wire)}. validate=${JSON.stringify(validate)}. Requirements: flag-off byte-identical (oracle SHA ${GOLDEN} unchanged), save PRE-CHECK data only (no faithfulness verdict persisted), reload re-verifies from scratch.
Ask codex: "Is the flag-off path provably byte-identical (write fully guarded by PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED, default unchanged, oracle SHA unchanged)? Does the checkpoint persist ONLY pre-check data (drafts/outline) and NEVER a faithfulness/strict_verify verdict, so a resumed run cannot trust a stale verdict? Does the reload path re-run verification from scratch? Any way a poisoned/partial checkpoint could corrupt a resumed run's faithfulness? End with: CHECKPOINT-SAFE or CHECKPOINT-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.`,
  { label: 'codex-gate', phase: 'Codex-Gate', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['verdict','codex_points'], properties:{ verdict:{type:'string'}, codex_points:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}} } } })

phase('Record')
const rec = await agent(
  `Record the 2C checkpoint wiring. Worktree ${WT} (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2c).
Only commit if validate passed (flag_off_byte_identical=${validate.flag_off_byte_identical}, collection_ok=${validate.collection_ok}, roundtrip_test_passes=${validate.roundtrip_test_passes}, no_verdict_persisted=${validate.no_verdict_persisted}) and codex=CHECKPOINT-SAFE (${gate.verdict}). Else commit nothing, report blocker.
1. Stage src/ + the new tests/test_checkpoint_roundtrip.py (NOT the overlaid tests/oracle/*). Grep-check no secret.
2. Write docs/review_readiness/checkpoint_2c.md: what was wired, the flag-off byte-identical proof (oracle SHA), the pre-check-data-only guarantee, the reload-re-verifies design, codex verdict.
3. Commit ("Phase 2C: wire generation checkpoint (pre-check data only, flag-gated, byte-identical when off)") with trailers:
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01TLpJSqNfJVSP1UGdtk89nA
4. Push -u origin chore/review-readiness-phase2c; open PR to base gate-inversion: '/home/polaris/.local/bin/gh pr create --repo aldrinor/deep-cove-research --base gate-inversion --head chore/review-readiness-phase2c --title "Code-review readiness: Phase 2C generation checkpoint (flag-gated, byte-identical off)" --body "Turns on the generation checkpoint: saves pre-check data only (drafts/outline, never a verdict), write gated behind PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED (default off = byte-identical, oracle-proven 9c0a3d43), reload re-verifies from scratch. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)"'.
Return {commit_sha, pushed, pr_url, committed_or_blocked}.`,
  { label: 'record', phase: 'Record', schema: { type:'object', additionalProperties:false, required:['commit_sha','pushed','pr_url','committed_or_blocked'], properties:{ commit_sha:{type:'string'}, pushed:{type:'boolean'}, pr_url:{type:'string'}, committed_or_blocked:{type:'string'} } } })

return { setup: { wiring_state: setup.wiring_state, save_point: setup.save_point }, wire, validate, gate, record: rec }

setup.wiring_state:
PARTIALLY WIRED (native LangGraph saver) + DORMANT (A2 rewind API + the specific 2C pre-check save).

Wired: In graph.py build_and_run() (pipeline B), get_checkpointer() is called at line 1428, entered as async CM at 1498 (_optional_checkpoint), and passed to graph.compile(checkpointer=...) at 1501-1502/1530. Resume is wired at 1539-1544 (has_checkpoint -> run_input=None so LangGraph resumes). So when PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED=1, LangGraph auto-saves full state after every node and can resume. This is the only place in src/ that imports checkpoint_manager (grep: only graph.py). Optional interrupt_before/interrupt_after are parsed at 1507-1528.

Dormant: (1) The A2 rewind API (list_checkpoints / get_checkpoint_state / rewind_to_checkpoint) has ZERO callers in src/. (2) There is NO purpose-built "save drafts/outline as pre-check data (never a verdict) before the faithfulness/strict_verify check" checkpoint — that Plan V4 item-2C save does not exist yet; the current mechanism only relies on LangGraph's generic per-node autosave. (3) In the honest_pipeline.py (pipeline A / benchmark) strict_verify path there is no checkpoint_manager integration at all — it is graph.py-only.
setup.save_point:
Two candidate loci depending on which pipeline 2C targets:

Pipeline B (graph.py, LangGraph): drafts/outline are produced in _synthesize (defined graph.py:861; outline at 888-893, report via synthesize_report/compose_from_wiki at 901-905). The faithfulness verdict is computed upstream in _verify (~line 518-530, sets result['faithfulness_score']) and the finalize gate is _should_finalize (graph.py:1188), routed from the conditional edge at 1274-1281. The 2C pre-check save belongs AFTER _synthesize returns its drafts/outline dict but BEFORE _should_finalize reads the verdict — i.e., persist result (sections/outline/final_report) as pre-check data only, not the pass/fail verdict.

Pipeline A (honest_pipeline.py, strict_verify): the draft arrives as the draft_text parameter (line 181, already produced by the caller); the strict_verify verdict is computed at honest_pipeline.py:344 (strict = strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, ...)). The exact 2C save-point is immediately BEFORE line 344 — after draft_text + evidence_pool are assembled (evidence_pool built at 343) and after Phase 3 contradiction detection (318-333), but before the strict_verify verdict. Save the draft/evidence_pool as pre-check data, never the strict_verify outcome.

wire.save_wiring: Save added in run_honest_pipeline immediately BEFORE the strict_verify verdict at src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py (the call `strict = strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, quantified_models=...)`, originally line 344). Right after `evidence_pool = {ev["evidence_id"]: ev for ev in evidence}` is built, a flag-gated block runs: `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(): _pg2c_save_precheck(run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool)`. _pg2c_save_precheck writes ONLY {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} as JSON to PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR/pg2c_precheck_{run_id}.json — never the strict_verify verdict. Verified at runtime: saved file keys are exactly [draft_text, evidence_pool, run_id], no strict/verdict/faithfulness field. Pipeline B (graph.py) was already fully wired (get_checkpointer at 1428, compile checkpointer at 1501-1502, LangGraph autosaves full pre-check ResearchState per node, verdict nodes re-run on resume) — left untouched.
wire.reload_wiring: Reload added at the TOP of run_honest_pipeline (src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py), right after `run_dir_path` is created and before the Phase 2b scope gate. Flag-gated: `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(): _pg2c_reloaded = _pg2c_load_precheck(run_id); if _pg2c_reloaded is not None: draft_text = _pg2c_reloaded.get("draft_text", draft_text)`. _pg2c_load_precheck returns only {draft_text, evidence_pool, run_id} (never a verdict). Execution then falls through normally to the Phase 4 step where strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, ...) runs FROM SCRATCH on the reloaded draft — the verdict is recomputed, never restored from disk. Three helper functions were added near the top of the module (after the logger): _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(), _pg2c_checkpoint_path(run_id), _pg2c_save_precheck(...), _pg2c_load_precheck(...). checkpoint_manager's AsyncSqliteSaver could not be reused here because pipeline A is a synchronous non-LangGraph orchestrator with no ResearchState/graph; the localized JSON helpers reuse the same resolve() accessor and PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR for consistency.
wire.collection_after: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 11.61s — matches the expected 16738/11 baseline exactly. The 11 collection errors are pre-existing and unrelated to this change. Flag-OFF is byte-identical: both the save and reload call sites are wrapped entirely in `if _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled():`, and _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled() returns `resolve("PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED") == "1"` with default '0' (config_defaults.py:97, default unchanged). Verified at runtime: flag default -> False, flag=1 -> True, flag=0 -> False. When the flag is off no checkpoint file is written, no new branch is taken, and draft_text is unmodified — no changed ordering. py_compile passed on the changed file.

validate.notes:
All four gates green on worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2c (branch chore/review-readiness-phase2c, HEAD c5448ec).

GATE 1 — FLAG-OFF byte-identical (critical): PASS. Oracle replay SHA = 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 (matches required golden exactly). "replay artifact BYTE-IDENTICAL to recorded golden" + "ACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid" (exit 0). Verified BOTH with PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED unset AND explicitly =0 — identical SHA both ways. Proves normal runs are unchanged when the flag is off.

GATE 2 — Collection unchanged: PASS. "16738 tests collected, 11 errors" — matches the expected 16738/11 baseline exactly. Oracle overlay cassettes present (acceptance_golden.json, acceptance_llm.jsonl, acceptance_retrieval.jsonl, demo_artifact.jsonl, demo_llm.jsonl).

GATE 3 — Config characterization: PASS. tests/test_config_registry.py + tests/test_settings_models.py = 9 passed, 0 failed.

GATE 4 — FLAG-ON roundtrip (NEW test): PASS, 4/4. Wrote /home/polaris/wt/phase2c/tests/test_checkpoint_roundtrip.py exercising the real Plan V4 2C generation-checkpoint wiring in src/polaris_graph/honest_pipeline.py (_pg2c_save_precheck / _pg2c_load_precheck), hermetic (no network; PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED=1 and PG_CHECKPOINT_DIR set via monkeypatch to tmp_path; strict_verify stubbed with a sentinel).
  (a) checkpoint artifact IS written (pg2c_precheck_<run_id>.json) and contains the drafts/outline — the draft_text (with '# Outline'/'## Section' headers) plus the evidence_pool. PASS.
  (b) artifact contains NO faithfulness/strict_verify verdict field: persisted keys are EXACTLY {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool}; asserted absence of faithfulness/faithfulness_score/faithfulness_pct/strict/strict_verify/verdict/kept_sentences/dropped_sentences/verification/verified/passed both structurally and as raw-byte substrings. PASS.
  (c) reload re-runs verification (does not read a verdict from disk): _pg2c_load_precheck returns pre-check data only (verdict-free); a sentinel-patched strict_verify is proven to be invoked exactly once on the reloaded draft — mirroring honest_pipeline.py Phase 4 (line 453) where strict_verify runs from scratch on the restored draft after reload (line 303-306). PASS.

NO VERDICT PERSISTED — confirmed. The 2C checkpoint is deliberately pre-check-only: honest_pipeline.py saves {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} BEFORE strict_verify is computed (line 445-453) and on reload restores only draft_text (line 303-306), then recomputes strict_verify at line 453. A poisoned/partial verdict can never be trusted from disk. The design comment at lines 70-80 and 297-302 documents this invariant, and the test locks it in.

Note on scope: honest_pipeline.py is a synchronous non-LangGraph orchestrator; its 2C checkpoint (JSON, pre-check only) is separate from the heavier LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver in src/polaris_graph/checkpoint_manager.py (A2 traceback/rewind). The task's roundtrip contract (drafts/outline saved, no verdict, reload re-verifies) maps precisely to the 2C JSON path, which is what the new test targets.

codex_points: CODEX (GPT-5.6, medium effort) returned CHECKPOINT-SAFE, exit 0. Evidence independently verified against worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase2c HEAD c5448ec before gating.

Q1 Flag-off byte-identical: YES. Both the read (line 303) and write (line 451) call sites are fully guarded by _pg2c_checkpoint_enabled(); config default PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED='0' unchanged (config_defaults.py:97); oracle SHA 9c0a3d43...db98 reproduced with the flag both unset and explicitly =0.

Q2 Pre-check data only: YES. _pg2c_save_precheck writes exactly {run_id, draft_text, evidence_pool} and runs BEFORE strict_verify (save at 451-452, strict_verify at 453), so the faithfulness verdict physically cannot be persisted. GATE 4 test asserts absence of every verdict-shaped key both structurally and as raw-byte substrings.

Q3 Reload re-verifies from scratch: YES. _pg2c_load_precheck restores only draft_text (line 306); strict_verify is then invoked fresh on that draft at line 453; the sentinel test confirms exactly one fresh invocation. No verdict is read from disk.

Q4 Poisoned/partial checkpoint cannot corrupt faithfulness: confirmed. Malformed JSON, missing draft_text key, and load errors all fall back to the supplied draft_text (a REQUIRED kwarg, so the .get() default is always valid); save errors are swallowed and never skip verification; stored evidence_pool is never restored so tampering is inert. A hostile draft swapped on disk can replace candidate content but must still pass fresh strict_verify and renders only from strict.kept_sentences — no verdict bypass.

Non-blocking robustness note (CODEX): _pg2c_load_precheck does not type-validate that draft_text is a str or that run_id matches; this is an availability/robustness concern only, not a path to restoring or bypassing a verdict. Does not block CHECKPOINT-SAFE.
Scope note: gate covers Pipeline A (honest_pipeline.py) synchronous JSON pre-check checkpoint only; Pipeline B LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver was left untouched.

summary: Turn on the generation checkpoint (2C): save pre-check data only, gate write behind PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED so flag-off runs stay byte-identical, reload re-verifies from scratch

phases.title: Setup
phases.detail: worktree + study checkpoint_manager.py + the generation loop

phases.title: Wire
phases.detail: save pre-check data (gated by flag) + reload path (re-verify from scratch)

phases.title: Validate
phases.detail: flag-off oracle byte-identical + collection unchanged + flag-on save/reload roundtrip test

phases.title: Codex-Gate
phases.detail: codex confirms flag-off byte-identical + no verdict persisted
